name: Guianese Creole
notes: STUB (deliberate): the grapheme inventory of French Guianese Creole is NOT specified. Kriyòl gwiyannen is a French-lexifier creole written in a phonologically-based, GEREC-influenced Latin orthography. The GRAPHEME SET is citable (French Wikipedia): vowels a, é, è, i, o, ò, ou; semivowels w, y; nasal vowels <an> and <en> (note: no <on> is listed, contrary to the Antillean norms); consonants b, ch, d, f, g, j, k, l, m, n, p, r, s, t, v, z, with c, h, q and x unused (q -> k, x -> z, silent h dropped); <r> is realised as French /ʁ/, not the Antillean [w]; <ch> = [ʃ] (Omniglot). But NO source consulted gives a per-grapheme IPA table: the fr/en Wikipedia articles list letters with example words only, and the Omniglot pronunciation chart is an image. Filling the vowel values by analogy with Haitian or Antillean creole spelling would be cross-language guessing, so no map is claimed. INPUT CONTRACT (for any future spec): lowercase Latin-script kriyòl gwiyannen in the GEREC-style orthography, diacritics (é è ò) intact.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Créole guyanais
sources.notes: Lists the alphabet (vowels a é è i o ò ou; semivowels w y; nasals an, en; consonants b ch d f g j k l m n p r s t v z) with example words but NO IPA column. Notes /ʁ/ is French-like, unlike the Antillean [w].

sources.author: Ager, Simon
sources.title: French Guianese Creole (kriyòl gwiyannen)
sources.notes: States Q and X are written K and Z, and C occurs only in the digraph CH = [ʃ]. The full alphabet/pronunciation chart is an image, so no letter->IPA table could be extracted.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Guianese Creole French.
